title: Camping with Colin's Family
summary:
Our next stop was meeting up with my parents on the Apache Trail, a windy and steep dirt road cutting through the Supersition Mountains between Phoenix and Roosevelt Lake. We arrived a few hours earlier than my parents and were tasked with finding a campsite that would be a good place for Maya and my parent's two dogs to play without being too close to the road. This proved more difficult than expected, but we eventually found a nice little pullout that gave us some privacy and had a little hiking trail leading out of it. When they arrived, Maya got reacquainted with Sasha and Keely (my parent's dogs) and we set up a very nice camp with some shade structures, comfy camping chairs and some tables for playing games.

The weekend was very nice and relaxing. We ate many yummy dinners including lentil soup, pasta with bolognese sauce and pan seared brats. The brats were supposed to be roasted over the fire but we forgot to bring firewood 😢. Before each dinner, we had a nice assortment of cheese and crackers and played Settlers of Catan. During the days, we went on hikes exploring up and down Fish Creek, a little stream that (unlike most 'rivers' in Arizona) had water running in it. On our hikes, we found some cool caves and the dogs had a great time chasing eachother and splashing around in the water. The crux of the weekend was the drive out, where we would have to make it up the Fish Creek Incline, a 1.5 mile stretch of dirt road the winds over 1000 feet up and out of the Fish Creek drainage. Despite the signs saying 4x4 required (our van is 2WD), numerous online blog posts state that any car can make it up or down the incline. Luckily the blog posters were correct, and we made it up the road without any hiccups. To celebrate, we cooked a taco breakfast with my parents at the overlook parking lot at the top of the incline.
